title: Async guard cancellation needs race coverage
learning: When a hook ignores stale async results, tests should simulate overlapping calls and assert the stale result cannot redirect or mutate state.

title: Preview DNS labels need the full deployed label length
learning: Branch slug truncation must account for the static deployment suffix in the same DNS label, not only the slug itself.

title: Deployment fixture tests must assert fixture presence
learning: Tests derived from safe-deployments data should assert that the expected fixture address or shared chain exists before comparing results, otherwise missing fixture data can turn the test into a no-op.

title: Partial new response shapes must preserve legacy findings
learning: Adding a new optional response object should not early-return and drop legacy findings or balance changes when the new object is partial.

title: E2E assertions must prove the intended action exists
learning: E2E helpers that expand a generic action and search the whole page can pass on the wrong action. Scope the assertion to the intended action and assert the action exists first.

title: Cypress selectors must match actual accessible labels
learning: Selectors using aria-label or visible text must match the accessible label in the actual flow, including tooltip-provided labels.

title: Confirming existing rejection transactions must keep nonce locked
learning: Existing cancellation transactions loaded from the queue must remain identity-locked; changing nonce in confirm flow creates a different transaction hash while users think they are confirming the original rejection.

title: Auth reconciliation must force fresh backend state
learning: Login/logout auth reconciliation that reads `/auth/me` must force a fresh request and avoid keeping a long-lived subscription, otherwise cached authenticated data can override the backend cookie state.

title: Wallet sign-in tests need unambiguous CTA selectors
learning: When multiple auth choices share copy such as “Continue with”, Cypress helpers must target the wallet CTA unambiguously or feature flags can send the test down the wrong auth path.

title: Repeated topbar shadows should be named once
learning: Repeated visual wrappers such as shadows and card chrome should be extracted to a component or named style when they appear in several nearby controls.

title: Mobile package internals can create duplicate provider contexts
learning: Importing a React Native UI hook from a package internals path can bypass the entrypoint used by the provider and create a separate context instance.

title: Conversion logic should be tested where it lives
learning: A component orchestration test that only asserts a converter was called does not test conversion correctness; conversion output belongs in the pure helper tests.

title: Back-merge docs must name the real source branch
learning: Fallback release instructions should not use placeholder-looking branch names. They need the real branch or generated branch pattern so operators do not create the wrong PR.

title: Default txOrigin must use the bounded helper
learning: Creating a default tx origin with raw JSON bypassed the existing length limit and could make encoded notes exceed downstream contracts. Use the canonical helper for bounded values.

title: New UI logic needs tests even when reused from an old design
learning: Redesigns that reuse old behavior still need tests for the new component, hook, slice, or branch that now owns the behavior.

title: Tx-flow submit registration must not hide alternatives
learning: Auto-selecting or setting submit state on mount can unregister other tx-flow submit options and leave the user unable to switch actions. Submit errors should set flow errors and reset loading instead of throwing.

title: Datadog error promotion should avoid SLO noise
learning: Caught exceptions should be warning-level unless they are genuinely user-impacting. Promoting polling failures or already-surfaced errors to RUM errors can flood SLOs and double-count the same incident.

title: Initial page spinners should not use refetch state
learning: Using `isFetching` for page gating hides already-loaded data during refetch. Use `isLoading` when the spinner represents only the initial load.

title: Hydration guards require explanation and tests
learning: When adding hydration/client-only guards, the PR should explain the need and add tests for the protected behavior rather than leaving the branch unproven.

title: Stable missing ENS results should not trigger repeated RPC calls
learning: If a resolver repeatedly queries for absent ENS names on every mount, stable null results need deliberate cache semantics to prevent avoidable RPC traffic and rate limits.

title: Visible balance semantics need product confirmation
learning: Changing a visible balance from all-chain sum to current-chain value is a product behavior change and should be explicitly confirmed or documented.

title: Workflow concurrency must include the ref
learning: A concurrency group keyed only by workflow name lets manual and scheduled runs on different refs cancel each other. Include the ref unless cross-ref cancellation is intentional.

title: Safe version metadata must be normalized before deployment lookups
learning: Safe versions can include suffixes such as `+L2`; deployment lookups must normalize versions before querying safe-deployments or EraVM/canonical detection can fail.

title: Contract resolution should use master-copy deployment data
learning: Aux-contract resolution should derive deployment type and chain-specific registration from the Safe master copy and safe-deployments data, update all web/mobile call sites, and handle undefined fallback results explicitly.

title: OIDC error maps need own-property lookup
learning: Redirect query values used as object keys can hit inherited properties such as `__proto__` or `toString`; map lookups should require own properties or use `Map`.

title: Long class compositions should move out of component bodies
learning: Very long responsive class compositions inside JSX can obscure component behavior; move them to a module/helper when readability suffers.

title: Chain selector eligibility should not be a naive set difference
learning: Listing add-network candidates by subtracting deployed chain IDs from all chains can expose incompatible chains and silent failures. Eligibility needs a compatibility hook and explicit unavailable states.

title: Multichain availability hooks need branch and story coverage
learning: Moving multichain eligibility into a new hook requires tests for loading, feature-disabled, unavailable, empty, already-deployed, and disabled-row branches, plus replacement Storybook coverage for removed states.

title: Removed hook return fields require consumer searches
learning: Removing a hook return field or prop such as `availableChains` requires searching all consumers and aligning mocks with the real prop contract so tests cannot pass with wrong props.

title: Disabled chain selector branch needs direct coverage
learning: When UI switches to a disabled branch during tx flow, add a test for that specific branch so the guard cannot be removed unnoticed.

title: Repeated scans should preserve discoveries for the open sheet
learning: When a screen promises discovery state for the lifetime of an open sheet, a later scan that finds no new items should not erase badges from an earlier scan unless that reset is intentional.

title: Skip behavior in data hooks needs focused tests
learning: Hooks with skip conditions need tests that assert the skip behavior directly, especially when the branch controls network calls or loading state.

title: Doc splits must preserve moved guidance
learning: Splitting a large agent or developer doc can silently drop important sections. Every removed section should be moved, intentionally superseded, or called out for review.

title: Hydration work needs justification and tests
learning: When adding hydration guards or client-only logic, the PR should explain why the logic is needed and add tests for the behavior it protects.

title: Settings explainers must branch on permission denial, not any failure
learning: Permission request flows can fail because registration or network work failed after permission was granted. Only actual denial/restriction should show the user a Settings explainer.

title: Merge priority needs per-source and per-chain tests
learning: New merge priority between space, private, and local contacts needs tests for source precedence and chain-level overlap so autocomplete and recipient resolution do not regress.

title: Pending collections must count toward non-empty UI
learning: Adding a collection that renders a tab or row requires updating empty-state predicates; otherwise real content can be hidden behind an empty state.

title: RTK mutations do not throw without unwrap
learning: RTK Query mutation triggers resolve with an object that can contain `error`. Success UI and local deletion must wait for `.unwrap()` or explicit `result.error` handling.

title: Address dedupe must preserve non-overlapping chain IDs
learning: Filtering contacts only by address can drop private contacts that contain chain IDs not covered by the higher-priority space contact. Dedupe needs to happen at the address and chain level.

title: Settings navigation must be explicit button tap only
learning: Permission-denied camera UI must not make the scan frame or wrapper open system Settings. Wrapper taps should be inert in denied/restricted states and only the labeled button should open Settings.

title: Long email addresses need sidebar overflow handling
learning: Long authenticated emails and names can overflow constrained sidebar profile UI. Treat them like wallet addresses: truncate or wrap intentionally.

title: Mount-only auth effects can miss hydrated session state
learning: A mount-only effect can capture initial `null` auth state before Redux persistence hydrates and then never observe the real expired session. Hydration-sensitive effects need the hydrated value or hydration flag in their dependencies.

title: Removed public exports must be removed from tests too
learning: Deleting a compatibility export while tests still call it breaks at runtime through namespace imports. Consumer and test searches must be part of public API removals.

title: Logout can race credentialed 403 session-expiry hooks
learning: Deliberate logout can race credentialed 403 handling after reload if persisted auth state is still present. Clear client auth before navigation or suppress session-expired handling during logout.

title: Append-style response hooks need idempotent initialization
learning: Migrating from a replace-style global hook to an append-style hook removes implicit idempotency. Initializers need an `initialized` guard or stored deregister callback.

title: Negative assertions must match current rendered text
learning: A negative text assertion that targets stale copy can pass even when the component regresses. Tests should assert against the text or test ID the component would actually render.

title: Safe app CTAs need loaded Safe state and deployment gating
learning: Global Safe App CTAs must derive the Safe from loaded Safe state rather than optional query params, and must hide or disable flows that blocked-route logic rejects for undeployed Safes.

title: Expo router layouts should not own business cleanup
learning: Mobile `_layout.tsx` files should stay focused on loading screens and navigation shell; cleanup such as WalletConnect disconnects belongs in the flow container or a dedicated hook.

title: Address helpers should live in shared utilities
learning: Before adding address normalization, validation, or truncation helpers in a screen/container, search shared utils and place reusable address helpers there so web/mobile callers use one implementation.

title: Fiat helpers should parse zero-like rates consistently
learning: Fiat conversion helpers must treat zero-like decimal strings such as "0.00" and invalid parsed rates as missing prices, not as visible $0.00 values.

title: Auth callbacks need server-verified session state
learning: Email/OIDC callbacks should mark the client authenticated only after a server-verified session signal succeeds, not from pending client flags alone.

title: Auth redirects should drop transient query state
learning: Default auth redirects should use a sanitized return path instead of carrying transient query params such as `error` into the post-login callback.

title: Feature internals should not import their own lazy barrel
learning: Feature-internal hooks should import direct feature flags, constants, or sibling modules instead of importing the feature barrel that exports the same hook and can create a circular dependency.

title: Release jobs need previous-state tag queries
learning: Release changelog jobs must compute the previous tag before creating the current tag; otherwise the current tag becomes the latest tag and the changelog range is empty.

title: Release duplicate checks cover all tag namespaces
learning: When introducing app-prefixed release tags while old unprefixed tags still exist, duplicate checks must inspect both namespaces before publishing artifacts derived from the shared version.

title: Lint-staged should use project-pinned tooling
learning: Git hooks and lint-staged commands should invoke the project-pinned formatter/test commands rather than a bare binary that can resolve a different transitive version than CI.

title: Mobile wallet integrations need config-aware provider placement
learning: Mobile wallet integrations should place providers below store/config context when initialization depends on Safes or supported chains, and should derive supported networks from app config rather than hardcoded subsets.

title: Mobile wallet icons should be hosted assets
learning: Mobile wallet icons and similar app assets should be hosted on an approved static asset domain instead of embedded as base64 data URIs that permanently increase startup bundle size.

title: Lazy feature groups must gate sibling chrome together
learning: When lazy feature components can render null while loading, surrounding chrome such as dividers must be gated on readiness too so users never see partial standalone UI.

title: Theme colors should not be hardcoded to patch palette drift
learning: If a design color differs from a generated theme variable, fix the token/generator choice or use an existing semantic/static token instead of hardcoding hex values in component CSS.

title: Near-duplicate sign-in buttons should fold into a configurable component
learning: Two sign-in buttons that differ only in label, icon, connection enum, analytics event, and test ID should be one configurable component (e.g. OidcSignInButton) that takes those differences as props, rather than two near-duplicate components each duplicating the feature-flag check, analytics call, and login redirect wiring.

title: Env-var defaults must match the format consumers parse
learning: Default values for env-var-driven config (e.g. allowed parent origins, CORS hosts, redirect allowlists) must match the exact format the consuming check expects — for origin allowlists, no trailing slash, since browser-supplied origins are slashless. Mismatched defaults silently fail strict checks even when the env var is unset.

title: E2E fiat assertions must match rendered currency spacing
learning: E2E fiat-value assertions must match the actual rendered output, including non-ASCII whitespace such as the thin Unicode space `FiatValue` inserts after the currency symbol; an ASCII-space-only regex like `/\$ [\d,]+/` produces false negatives on correctly rendered values.

title: E2E test must assert the specific behavior its description claims
learning: When a test description claims to validate a computed value (e.g. "correct sum of all protocol fiat values"), the assertion must actually verify that computation; merely checking that any matching value is visible lets regressions in the computation pass silently.

title: Extract embedded error boundaries from unrelated UI components
learning: Error-boundary class components should live in their own file rather than being colocated inside a specific UI component (e.g. a badge); the boundary is a cross-cutting concern that other consumers may need, and embedding it muddies the component's responsibility.

title: OIDC logout endpoints must be invoked via top-level browser navigation
learning: Auth endpoints that issue an HTTP redirect (e.g. /v1/auth/logout/redirect returning 303) and are meant to clear an upstream IdP session must be triggered with a top-level browser navigation (window.location or a real form submission), not via fetch/RTK Query. Calling them with fetch returns 200 to the SPA but never propagates the IdP-side session clear, so OIDC users get auto-signed-in on the next attempt. Also remove the form synchronously only after navigation has begun, otherwise the POST can be cancelled before reaching the gateway.

title: Unwrap RTK mutations before mutating auth state on success
learning: Awaiting an RTK Query mutation does not throw on HTTP/API failures unless .unwrap() is used. When a follow-up dispatch represents the success path (e.g. clearing local auth after logout), call .unwrap() so failed responses skip the success branch and reach the catch path; otherwise client state desynchronises from server state on any backend error.

title: Reconciliation errors must still drive the user to a safe terminal state
learning: If the post-logout reconciliation request fails transiently (5xx, network), only logging the error and clearing the in-progress flag leaves Redux marked authenticated forever; route guards then keep the UI in a signed-in state with no retry. Treat a transient reconcile error the same as 'authenticated reconciliation' for the deliberate-logout path: force the user to unauthenticated so they re-login.

title: Mock platform data sources to validate intent, not fixture state
learning: When a behavior depends on a third-party data source returning 'not found' (e.g. safe-deployments missing a chain), do not rely on the chain genuinely being absent for the test to be meaningful, the data source can change. Mock the lookup to return undefined so the test consistently validates the intended fallback branch. Also add coverage for the explicit-null variant of the input to lock current behavior.

title: Drop-in component replacements must preserve the original prop surface
learning: When swapping a third-party component for an in-house implementation under the same export name, the new component must accept and honor the props the old one supported (here, Moti's `show` for forced visibility). Hard-coding visibility from internal state silently changes behavior at every call site that passed the original prop, which is exactly the kind of regression that 'drop-in replacement' framing is meant to prevent.

title: Reusable onboarding components must not hard-code Back navigation
learning: An onboarding screen mounted under multiple routes (e.g. /welcome and /spaces/create-space) cannot hard-code its Back button to one of those paths, that breaks navigation expectations from the other entry. Use history back or derive the target from the current pathname so the control behaves correctly in every context where the component is reused.

title: Topbar rewrites must port over icons users rely on
learning: When rebuilding a shared shell (header, sidebar, etc.) from a new design that omits existing affordances, walk the old shell side-by-side and explicitly decide what to keep. The Spaces topbar PR shipped without the connected-dApp indicator and the Staking entry that were live in production because they were not in Figma; doing a delta against the old surface catches these gaps before review.

title: Removing config files must update the docs that reference them
learning: When deleting a config file (.claude/settings.json) that contributor docs reference (AGENTS.md still documenting the Stop hook + SKIP_VERIFY=1 escape hatch), update those docs in the same PR and remove any now-orphaned scripts. Otherwise contributors and agents expect a verification run that no longer happens.

title: Empty-state gating must include all visible collections
learning: When a tabbed page can render multiple sources (e.g., workspace contacts plus locally stored ones), gating the empty state on a single collection's length hides the other tab's contents. The empty-state condition must consider every collection rendered by the page.

title: Deduplicate aggregated chain IDs when merging address rows
learning: Aggregating chainIds across local address books with array push allows duplicate entries when the same chain/address appears multiple times. Use a Set during aggregation to avoid duplicate logos and incorrect 'all-chains' detection.

title: Local-only address-book rows must hide server-backed actions
learning: When admin actions call space-address-book endpoints, rendering them on rows that only exist in local storage causes Edit/Delete to fail. Hide server-backed actions for local-only rows or first promote the entry into the server-side address book.

title: Skip member queries until space ID is resolved
learning: Gating an RTK Query only on authentication while passing an unresolved or NaN spaceId issues failing requests during route hydration. Add a space-id validity guard to the `skip` option so queries do not fire with `0`/`NaN` IDs.

title: Do not duplicate generated SDK config files in app source
learning: Maintaining a near-copy of a shared package config (e.g. apps/web/src/config/chains.ts vs packages/utils/src/config/chains.ts) drifts silently. Import the shared definition rather than maintaining a parallel file.

title: Import from package public entrypoints, not deep dist paths
learning: Reaching into a dependency's `dist/src/...` paths is fragile across version bumps and produces awkward duplicated path segments (e.g. `utils/utils/...`). Import from the package's public entrypoint, and consolidate same-package imports into one statement.

title: URL-driven selectors should fall back to existing app state when URL is absent
learning: When switching a UI selector (e.g. topbar Safe selector) from Redux to URL as the source of truth, account for routes where the entity is not in the URL by falling back to the previous source rather than rendering an empty/default selector.

title: Extract shared dialog form when only mutation/label diverge
learning: When a new dialog form (e.g. AddPrivateContact) is a near-duplicate of an existing one (AddContact) with only the mutation hook, button label, and an extra description differing, extend the existing component (which already accepts a `label` prop) to take the mutation/body instead of forking. Forking duplicates form layout, state handling, and loading/error flow — DRY guidance in CLAUDE.md applies.

title: Resolve Safe display names per chain across full deployment, not first-network-only
learning: QA found that a Safe deployed to multiple chains showed only the first network's local-AB name; if that network had no entry, it fell back to the short address even though other networks had local names. When a Safe identity spans chains, name fallback resolution must iterate every deployment chain (or use a chain-aware lookup), not silently bind to the first chain in the list.

title: Pre-fetch unit tests for hooks that perform user-facing data fetching
learning: Reviewer flagged that a new hook orchestrating fetch + filter logic landed without unit tests. New hooks that do data orchestration (gating, sequencing, filtering) need their own unit tests covering the loading/empty/error branches the hook owns; reviewer-requested tests should not be deferred to follow-ups.

title: Test the debounce, not just the post-debounce expectation
learning: Copilot flagged that a `waitFor(() => expect(result.current.ens).toBeUndefined())` test passed immediately because the assertion was already true on first render — it never proved the staleness guard worked after the 200ms debounce; QA then found the bug was still present. Tests for debounced/throttled state must use fake timers (or otherwise advance time past the debounce) and assert that the lookup was actually called for the new input before checking the post-settle value.

title: Move large/complex Tailwind class strings into CSS modules
learning: Reviewer pushed back on a `className` containing 20+ utilities including arbitrary value chains and webkit-scrollbar variants, which are unreadable inline and undermine review. When a Tailwind class string outgrows ~5-6 tokens or contains arbitrary-value chains, move it into a CSS module (or named utility) so the JSX stays scannable.

title: Stick to one styling system per surface (MUI vs Tailwind), keep CSS modules for MUI layouts
learning: Reviewer suggested replacing CSS module padding with `pl-3` and using `@apply bg-secondary` in a MUI-rooted component; author explained the file is in the MUI subtree where Tailwind isn't configured, so `@apply` errors and CSS modules are the right tool. Decide which subtree owns a component (MUI/CSS modules vs Tailwind/shadcn) and don't mix utilities into the wrong system.

title: Document and bound z-index ranges across the app
learning: Reviewer asked why a sidebar z-index was set to 1250 instead of a small 0-9 range; author justified it against existing layers (Wallet popup 1300, notifications 2000) and acknowledged the codebase needs a unified z-index scale. New z-index values should be picked against a documented scale of existing layers, not invented per-component.

title: Delegate to existing utility instead of duplicating capability mapping
learning: Copilot flagged a new web-side hook re-declaring the `NativeTokenDisplay` type and SHOW_ALL/HIDE_NATIVE constants that already existed in `packages/utils` via `getNativeTokenDisplay()`, undermining the single-source-of-truth goal. Author imported the shared type/util instead. When introducing a hook that wraps a feature flag, delegate to the existing pure utility for the mapping rather than redeclaring the types and constants on the consumer side.

title: Keep refactors out of test-strategy / scaffolding PRs
learning: A test-infrastructure PR contained an unrelated `GlobalPushNotifications` utils extraction and typo fix; reviewer requested the refactor be removed and shipped separately, and the author reverted it. PRs whose primary purpose is tooling/test infrastructure must not bundle production-code refactors — split them so each can be reviewed for its own risk.

title: Drive test mock data from shared MSW fixtures
learning: Reviewer pointed out that a balances test still hand-rolled its own mock objects while the same PR introduced shared MSW fixtures (`balancesFixtures.efSafe`, `balancesFixtures.empty`) and builders (`balancesBuilder`, `balanceBuilder`, `erc20TokenBuilder`); author refactored the test to consume the shared fixtures. When a PR establishes shared test fixtures/builders, all touched tests must adopt them so the codebase has one source of truth for mocked API shapes.

title: Compose hook flows in their own composer, don't sprinkle gating across consumers
learning: Reviewer noted that a new EIP-712 message threat-analysis hook was being called at the top-level consumer next to the transaction analysis hook, instead of inside the existing `useThreatAnalysisHypernative` composer; author moved it inside. When extending a domain pipeline (analysis, validation, fetching), the new step belongs inside the composer hook, so consumers continue to use a single entry point with one gating decision.

title: Reset context state when its precondition flips false
learning: Reviewer flagged that context values were set when `isEip712` was true but never cleared when it became false, leaving stale data that could feed an incorrect downstream threat analysis; author added the cleanup. When a useEffect writes derived state into a context conditionally, the false branch must also reset the prior values.

title: Restrict `pull_request_target` workflows to same-repo branches
learning: Copilot warned that broadening a release-tag workflow to also fire for closed-but-unmerged PRs whose branch starts with `release` widens the trigger surface on a `pull_request_target` workflow with `contents: write`; the safe-guard is to also require `github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository`. Any expansion of `pull_request_target` triggers must keep a same-repo check (and ideally an actor allowlist) so forks cannot spoof a release-branch close.

title: Reuse existing analytics primitives — don't re-stringify error fields
learning: Reviewer pointed at an analytics call that built error context manually instead of using the shared `MixpanelEventParams.FAILURE_REASON` key; author adopted the existing param. New analytics call sites must use the existing `MixpanelEventParams` enum so dashboards group on the same field name.

title: Drop analysis data when its fetch errored
learning: Codex (accepted by author with a follow-up commit) flagged that a new `deadlockResults` selector returned cached data even when the underlying mutation reported `fetchError`, while sibling selectors for recipient/contract analysis correctly suppressed data on error — leaving a stale critical deadlock warning that blocked users while checks were actually unavailable. New result selectors in a multi-source analysis pipeline must guard on `fetchError` the same way as their siblings.

title: Cascading delays must propagate to every gated branch
learning: Codex flagged (and tmjssz seconded) that adding a deadlock signal lengthened the threat-card delay but the simulation-card delay branches were still gated only on `contractEmpty || recipientEmpty`, so simulation appeared before deadlock and reversed the intended severity-first reveal order. When introducing a new severity tier into a sequenced reveal, audit every dependent delay branch and merge overlapping conditionals so delays remain consistent.

title: Add a comparison helper instead of inlining priority-table arithmetic
learning: Reviewer suggested replacing inline `SEVERITY_PRIORITY[a] <= SEVERITY_PRIORITY[b]` checks with an `isSeverityHigherOrEqual` util so the same comparison can be used for `hasCriticalThreat` and `hasCriticalDeadlock`; author added it. When two branches in the same file repeat the same priority-table comparison, extract a named helper so the comparison reads as a domain operation, not arithmetic.

title: Extend JSDoc when the contract of a function changes
learning: Reviewer asked the author to update the JSDoc on `getOverallStatus` once a new analysis source (deadlock) became part of the function's inputs and decision; author updated it. When a public function gains a new input or output condition, its JSDoc must be updated in the same PR — stale docs on a Brain-Method-flagged function compound the maintenance burden.

title: Don't unconditionally show $0 for undeployed Safes when an aggregator can return real balances
learning: Reviewer caught that a `shouldHideNativeTokenValue` branch returned `<FiatValue value="0" />` for undeployed Safes regardless of whether Zerion was enabled — but Zerion can show non-native ERC-20 balances for counterfactual accounts, so the unconditional zero misrepresented the Safe's actual value. UI branches that hide a single token must still respect aggregators that surface other tokens for the same Safe.

title: Consolidate per-element Tailwind into the CSS module class when both are present
learning: When a component already gets a feature-specific CSS module class via `cn(...)`, do not keep a parallel inline-style or repeated Tailwind list for the same element. Pull the remaining utilities into the existing CSS module rule so styling lives in one place per element.

title: Keep test data builders separate from MSW storybook fixtures
learning: Builders under `apps/web/src/tests/builders/*` and MSW fixtures under `config/test/msw/fixtures/*` are not interchangeable. Builders are randomized, composable test data for unit tests where you control specific fields via `.with({ ... })`. MSW fixtures are large real CGW responses pinned to specific Safes for visual/integration tests. Unit tests should use builders, not import MSW fixtures.

title: Watch the bundle when introducing SVG icons; prefer CSS mask over next/image for static logos
learning: Importing an SVG as `import SafeLogo from '@/public/images/logo-no-text.svg'` and rendering it via `next/image` inlines the asset into the main bundle and can add ~140 KB. For a small static, themable logo, render it via a CSS `mask-image` against `currentColor` instead so the bytes stay in `/public` and only the CSS rule ships.

title: Collapse redundant router-guard rules; one branching action beats two siblings
learning: When adding a new auth-redirect rule, scan the surrounding rules: if a more general rule (e.g. `!isSiweAuthenticated → /welcome`) already fires for the same case, do not add a sibling rule with a stricter `match`. Branch the existing rule's `action` on the path instead so there is one source of truth for the redirect.

title: Extract Safe-item construction with many shared dependencies into a local closure or factory
learning: If a builder helper takes 6+ positional inputs that all originate from the same hook scope, that signature is a smell. Either close over the shared inputs in a local builder lambda inside the hook, or pass a single `deps` object. Six positional args make call sites unreadable and hide which dependencies actually drive memoization.

title: Hoist hook-stable computations out of the JSX render expression
learning: Computations whose inputs are stable for the lifetime of a render (e.g. `getAvatarColor(selectedSpace.id)`) should be assigned once before the JSX, not inlined into each element's `style` prop. This makes diffs cleaner, avoids recomputing per render slot, and surfaces dependency tracking when the value is later memoized.

title: Hoist regex literals out of hot loops
learning: When a regex literal is used inside a `for` loop body, define it once outside the loop. Each `/pattern/.test(x)` inside the loop allocates a fresh RegExp; for hex-data rendering this matters because the parts array can be hundreds of items long.

title: Replace long `!==` chains with set membership and named state values
learning: Conditions like `state !== 'unknown' && state !== 'invalid' && state !== 'known-other-chain' && state !== 'self-send'` are hard to scan and grow on every new state. Promote the disallowed list to a named array (or constant), use `!DISALLOWED.includes(state)`, and extract the resulting boolean into a well-named local before the JSX.

title: Coordinate UI-component edits with in-flight design-system PRs
learning: Do not patch shared shadcn primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` while a larger design-system rewrite of those same files is in flight. The merge will conflict and the local changes will be lost or duplicated. Land the design-system PR first, then apply the token swap on top.

title: Use the existing `sameAddress` helper everywhere, not ad-hoc `toLowerCase()` comparisons
learning: When comparing Ethereum addresses, always go through the `sameAddress(a, b)` utility. Mixing `addr.toLowerCase() === other.toLowerCase()` with `sameAddress(...)` in the same module makes review harder and risks one site silently disagreeing with the rest of the codebase on what 'equal' means.

title: Distinguish empty, errored, and unknown data states explicitly in fallback aggregators
learning: When a feature falls back from a richer source (portfolio) to a poorer one (tx-service), the fallback shape must keep three states distinguishable: known-empty (`positions: []`), unknown (`positions: undefined`), and errored (data is undefined and `error` is set). Hardcoding `positions: []` in the aggregator collapses errored into known-empty; keeping `undefined` for empty collapses known-empty into unknown and produces an infinite skeleton. Thread an `isPortfolioEmpty` flag (only true when the portfolio successfully returned empty data) into the aggregator and overlay `[]` only on that branch.

title: Pin Datadog SDK config that you cannot rotate at runtime
learning: For mobile telemetry knobs that cannot be flipped without a fresh release (verbosity, upload frequency, batch size, sample rate, native crash reporting), prefer hardcoded debug/production branches in the provider config over env vars. An env var the operator cannot actually change in production is misleading configuration surface.

title: Reset Datadog user context when the active Safe goes away
learning: Effects that write Safe-scoped context to a telemetry SDK (`DdSdkReactNative.addUserExtraInfo({ safeAddress, chainId })`) must also clear that context when the active Safe becomes null — e.g. after the user removes the last account. Otherwise subsequent RUM events stay tagged with the previous Safe and misattribute analytics.

title: Place feature subfolders next to their consumers when usage is local
learning: When a hook or helper is only consumed inside one component within a feature, colocate it in a subfolder of that component (e.g. SpaceSafeBar/hooks/) rather than scattering it elsewhere; same applies to inner sub-components of a flow.

title: Block transaction submission until token decimals are confirmed loaded
learning: Never fall back to a hardcoded decimals value (e.g. 18) when the balances query is loading or errored: ERC-20 tokens with non-18 decimals (USDC=6 etc.) will be encoded with the wrong unit scale. Expose an explicit isTokenDataReady flag that gates form validity / submit buttons.

title: Use a synchronous ref guard, not a state flag, against double-submit
learning: Submission guards based on `isSubmitting` state can be entered twice within a single render cycle (rapid taps). Use a `useRef` flag that is set synchronously before any await, and clear it only after completion, to make handlers re-entrant-safe.

title: Format intermediate decimal math without scientific notation
learning: When deriving a token amount from fiat (or any small division), `(a / b).toString()` emits scientific notation (e.g. `1.6e-7`) which downstream parsers like `safeParseUnits` reject. Format as a plain decimal string before storing or passing along.

title: Inside a feature, import internals via relative paths, not the feature barrel
learning: A feature's own components must not import from the feature's public barrel (`@/features/<name>`) — that creates a circular import and forces the eager bundle to include lazy chunks. Use relative paths (`../hooks/...`, `../store/...`) for in-feature imports.

title: Clear error state when retrying an async verification
learning: When a retry handler resets the underlying widget/request, also call `setError(null)`; otherwise the UI still branches on the old error and shows a stale failure message during the retry attempt.

title: Use sameAddress() for address comparisons across the codebase
learning: Inline `.toLowerCase()` comparisons for Ethereum addresses are fragile: they assume a particular checksum convention and miss the existing utility. Always import and use `sameAddress` from the utils package for owner/recipient/token-address matching.

title: Remove deprecation markers when reverting to the previously-deprecated component
learning: When a refactor or rollback brings back a component that had a `@deprecated` JSDoc annotation, delete the annotation in the same PR. Leaving it in place creates confusion about which component is canonical and risks future churn.

title: Mount navigators before running navigation effects
learning: If a layout returns `null` while `isLoading`, do not call `router.replace()` from a child effect — there is no navigator mounted to consume the action and module-level guards (`navigated = true`) prevent the retry once the navigator finally mounts.

title: E2E assertions and their comments must agree
learning: When a test comment describes the expected behavior (e.g. 'should still be visible'), the assertion must match it. A mismatch (assertNotVisible vs 'should be visible') means the test would not catch the regression it claims to cover, and silently masks bugs.

title: Centralize transaction-derived analyses in the SafeShield context
learning: Avoid leaking analysis-specific props (e.g. deadlock params) into ExecuteForm/SignForm and avoid having each Review flow push state into a context. SafeShield already has `safeTx` and `txDetails`; derive owner/threshold projections in the context itself, and route blocking statuses through the existing `needsRiskConfirmation` mechanism instead of creating a parallel blocking flag.

title: RTK Query persistence filter paths must match serialized cache keys
learning: Persistence transforms or store filters that read into RTK Query's `queries` slice must use the exact serialized cache key, including the surrounding quotes that RTK Query adds for string arguments. Switching an endpoint from `getX(undefined)` to `getX(arg)` changes the key to `getX("arg")`, and template literals like `getX(${KEY})` produce `getX(KEY)` (no quotes), so `lodash.get` paths and store-query keys silently miss the entry.

title: Auto-exporting NEXT_PUBLIC secrets must handle multi-line values, digits, and empty sets
learning: When a CI step replaces the per-key `env:` block with a single `jq`-driven export to `$GITHUB_ENV`, it must use heredoc-style delimiters for multi-line values (some `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` secrets are JSON), include digits in env-name regexes, and avoid making a conditional `[ -n "$X" ] && echo` the last command of a `set -euo pipefail` script — otherwise an empty value collapses the script's exit code to 1 and breaks the build.

title: Replacing a layout component must port over every side effect of the old one
learning: When a global header/layout is replaced with a new variant (Header → Topbar), each entry point of the old component must be re-implemented or explicitly removed: the sidebar opener (`setSidebarOpen`), the batch toggle (`setBatchOpen`), and ambient hooks like `useShowNotificationsRenewalMessage` were lost when only the visible chrome was migrated, leaving features unreachable in production.

title: Read live state from selectors, not from child component refs
learning: A parent that needs to display reactive child-derived state (unread count, computed totals) must subscribe to the underlying store/selector itself rather than reading `ref.current.value` during render. Refs do not trigger re-renders when their fields change, so badges read this way start at the initial value and never update.

title: isLoading must be the sole driver of skeleton UI
learning: Conditions like `isLoading || (!error && !data)` conflate `still fetching` with `loaded but empty/unavailable`. The data layer must guarantee `isLoading` is true during initial fetch, and the UI must rely on `isLoading` alone for the skeleton; rendering the skeleton when data is `undefined` produces an infinite loader on any code path that legitimately returns `undefined`.

title: Place new screen components inside their owning feature folder
learning: New widgets or list components belong inside an existing top-level feature folder (`features/transactions/components/PendingTxList`, `features/assets/components/AssetsList`) rather than getting their own feature for each component. Promote a folder to a feature only when it owns a contract or page boundary; otherwise the feature graph fragments.

title: Cross-feature consumption must go through useLoadFeature
learning: When one feature mounts a component owned by another feature, it must pull the component through `useLoadFeature(<OtherFeature>)` rather than importing it directly. Direct imports defeat the lazy-loading boundary the feature architecture was designed for and inflate the main bundle.

title: Preserve existing data-testid hooks on QA-instrumented elements
learning: When restructuring a dashboard or widget, keep every existing `data-testid` attribute on the same logical element. The QA E2E suite addresses these IDs directly, and silently moving or dropping them breaks the test suite without surfacing a build error.

title: Forward auto-mode to native appearance APIs as null
learning: When syncing the app theme into React Native's `Appearance.setColorScheme`, auto mode must pass `null` rather than the resolved scheme. Writing `light`/`dark` overrides the OS source `useColorScheme()` reads, so subsequent OS theme changes stop propagating and `auto` no longer follows the system.

title: Reuse the existing label formatter instead of re-implementing decimal rounding
learning: Before adding a new `formatAmounts*`/`formatNumber*` helper, check the transactions/utils module for an existing function with matching semantics. Adding a parallel formatter creates two divergent rounding rules in the codebase even when the new use case looks superficially different.

title: restrict postMessage target origin in AppCommunicator
learning: Validate Safe App URLs before constructing origin: skip when missing, try/catch new URL(), and refuse iframe messages whose payload shape (e.g. an isCookieEnabled flag) was being used as an early-return bypass past origin/source checks.

title: manually edited spaces.ts AUTO_GENERATED + missing safeCount
learning: When a backend response shape changes, update packages/store schema.json and regenerate; do not hand-edit AUTO_GENERATED types. Hand edits silently revert on next codegen and consumers crash when the field is undefined at runtime.

title: Spaces feature barrel circular imports
learning: Internal feature files must use relative imports (./hooks/useFoo) for siblings; importing from the feature's own barrel (@/features/spaces) creates circular dependencies that work today but break under different bundlers/test runners and re-export ordering changes.

title: localStorage seeding overwrites visited safe
learning: When a Cypress helper overwrites a whole localStorage key (vs merging), every fixture that the test depends on must include the auto-trusted entries the app populates on visit, or the tests silently get a non-pinned-warning banner across many specs.

title: multichain_sidebar.cy.js timeout option
learning: In Cypress, `.should('equal', value, { timeout })` parses the third argument as the assertion message, not a retry timeout. Pass timeout to the preceding cy.* command instead.

title: useFlowActivationGuard PUBLIC_ROUTES
learning: Prefix matching for public/private routes must compare pathname.startsWith(route), not route.startsWith(pathname). Reversing the direction misclassifies short pathnames as matching long route prefixes.

title: NamedAddressInfo replaced ENS-only resolver
learning: Don't substitute a richer presentation component for a narrowly-scoped one without verifying every fallback path. NamedAddressInfo resolves both ENS and Etherscan contract names via the gateway, so it surfaced 'Proxy' for unnamed/un-ENS'd safes when the network changed — a regression that required reverting in production.

title: useTotalBalances vs useRefetchBalances
learning: useTotalBalances forced fiatCode to uppercase, while useRefetchBalances kept the lowercase persisted value. RTK Query treated USD vs usd as distinct cache keys, so manual refetches updated a different entry than the one the UI rendered, leaving stale balances after transactions.

title: zkSync canonical multisend
learning: Deployment matching that hardcodes one zkSync chain id, treats canonical address as deployed on every chain, and forwards version metadata into deployment lookups misses canonical Safes on sibling zkSync networks and on +L2-tagged versions.

title: checkbox replaced theme token with hardcoded color
learning: When porting MUI components to shadcn/Tailwind, keep the theme token (border-input) instead of substituting a literal color (border-gray-300). Hardcoded colors break dark mode and bypass the design-token escape hatch.

title: store-codegen-check.yml
learning: Drift check used `git diff --exit-code` which ignores untracked files, and ran `build:dev` which fetches the remote schema, masking schema/codegen mismatches.

title: encodeEIP1271Signature reimplemented
learning: Before adding low-level cryptography helpers (EIP-1271 signature encoding, signing libraries, contract address derivation), check the Safe SDKs/utils packages — there is usually a tested implementation already exposed there.

title: continue-on-error masks Cypress regressions
learning: Setting `continue-on-error: true` on a smoke-test job to keep a downstream artifact-upload step running converts test failures into successful workflow runs. Use `if: !cancelled()` on the downstream job instead, so the test job preserves its real failure status.

title: Cypress feature-flag intercept missed paginated chains response
learning: When mocking gateway responses for visual/E2E tests, mutate the actual response shape. /v1/chains is paginated as { results: Chain[] } and feature flags live on each results[i].features — top-level body.features mutation is a no-op and the visual tests still depend on live backend flags.

title: direct TxHistoryContainer broke Tabs context
learning: Don't replace a Tabs.Container/Tabs.Tab provider with a leaf container when descendants still use Tabs.FlashList — they need the tab context. Either keep a single-tab container provider or swap the descendant list for a non-tab variant.

title: formatPercentage with signDisplay='never'
learning: When a shared formatter is configured with signDisplay='never', the caller is responsible for emitting the sign for both positive AND negative values — relying on color alone misstates the numeric value to colorblind/screen-reader users.

title: useParentSafeThreshold reverted
learning: Don't hardcode `nestedSafeOwners?.[0]` as the parent safe when resolving threshold for a delegator under edit/delete — the parent must be derived from the actual delegator address. Hardcoding fetches the wrong parent's threshold and produces signatures attributable to the wrong delegator. Address comparisons in proposer permission checks must remain checksum-insensitive (`sameAddress`), not strict ===.

title: Spaces navigation drifted when helpers fell back to the first Space before hydration
learning: A Spaces URL-sync hook could run before redux-persist hydration, treat a still-rehydrating signed-in user as signed out, and redirect a deep link into the welcome flow. Separately, Safe-switch route builders dropped `spaceId`, while `useCurrentSpaceId` silently fell back to the first owned Space; the combination moved users into the wrong workspace after switching Safes. Effects that redirect from persisted auth state must wait for hydration, and Space-scoped navigation should preserve the URL `spaceId` instead of inventing a fallback active Space.

title: Classic-view opt-in bypassed the require-login gate even after sign-in
learning: A session opt-in (`isClassicViewOptedIn`) short-circuited `useIsRequireLoginEnabled` unconditionally, so a user who opted into classic view and then signed in within the same tab kept bypassing the gate and its onboarding redirects. An opt-in or escape-hatch that overrides an auth gate must be scoped to signed-out sessions: re-check `isAuthenticated` (or clear the opt-in on sign-in) so authenticating re-enables the gate.

title: Coercing a still-loading feature flag to false fired routing before it resolved
learning: `useIsRequireLoginEnabled()` returns `boolean | undefined` (undefined while chains config loads), but call sites coerced it with `?? false`. That treated the gate as OFF on first mount, firing legacy redirects that dropped the `?next=` round-trip, flashing the legacy welcome screen, and skipping post-sign-in forwarding when SIWE resolved before the flag. Handle the tri-state explicitly: an `undefined` (still-resolving) flag must mean "wait / allow until known", never a hard `false`. Gate redirects, `return null` loaders, and no-header lists on `=== true` / `=== false`, not on a coerced boolean.

title: Fire-and-forget async handlers leaked unhandled promise rejections
learning: An un-awaited `disconnect()` and an `onClick={onFinish}` whose `await submit().unwrap()` could reject both surfaced as unhandled promise rejections (console/Sentry noise), even though a visible error alert already existed. Any fire-and-forget async call (event-handler `onClick`, cleanup, bail-out branch) must either be awaited inside a `try/catch` or wrapped in an awaited IIFE that logs/handles rejection, so the user-facing error state is the only path and no rejection escapes.

title: Number(spaceId) silently produced NaN and hit the API with it
learning: `spaceId` is `string | null` from auth state; `Number(spaceId)` yields `NaN` for empty/whitespace/non-numeric values and that `NaN` was passed straight into space-scoped API calls. Parse external string-to-number conversions through a guarded helper (`parseSpaceId` returning `null` for non-finite results) and skip the call when parsing fails, rather than trusting raw `Number()` coercion at the call site.

title: FE DTO marked backend-optional fields required; null coercion scattered
learning: A hand-written `BackendCfSafeDto` typed `fallbackHandler`/`to`/`paymentReceiver` as required strings while the backend schema marks them optional/nullable, so a null from the API would violate the FE type and downstream address code. Align the DTO optionality with the backend schema (`?: string | null`) and centralize the `null → ZERO_ADDRESS` normalization in the mapper (`fromBackendDto`) instead of coercing per call site.

title: permissions:{} workflows missing per-job contents:read break checkout
learning: Setting workflow-level `permissions: {}` (least privilege) removes the inherited `contents: read` scope, but every job that runs `actions/checkout` still needs it. Jobs that don't re-declare `contents: read` fail checkout with an insufficient-token-scope error. When tightening a workflow to `permissions: {}`, add an explicit minimal `permissions:` block (including `contents: read`) to each job that checks out the repo.

title: Number() of a balance string collapsed unknown and whitespace into $0
learning: `formatBalance` ran `Number(fiatTotal)` directly, but `Number('   ') === 0`, so whitespace-only / effectively-empty gateway strings rendered as a known `$0` instead of the unknown-value dash. Guard numeric coercion of external strings: reject `null`/`undefined`, `trim()` and treat `'' → unknown`, then `Number.isFinite` the parsed value — keep the "known zero vs unknown/unloaded" distinction explicit rather than letting coercion erase it.

title: threshold <= 1 let an invalid zero threshold pass as ready-to-execute
learning: `threshold <= 1 ? 'AWAITING_EXECUTION' : 'AWAITING_CONFIRMATIONS'` silently mapped an invalid `threshold === 0` to the ready-to-execute state. When a value has a known valid floor, use the exact comparison (`=== 1`) so malformed inputs surface instead of being absorbed by an over-broad bound.

title: Dirty/can-save derived from RTK cache re-enabled during post-mutation refetch
learning: `isDirty`/`canSave` was computed against the RTK Query cache (`trimmedName !== space.name`). After a successful save the cache still holds the old value until the refetch settles, so Save re-enabled in that window and allowed a duplicate submission. Derived "dirty"/"can-submit" state must not be recomputed from server cache during the post-mutation refetch window: track a local saved-baseline or gate on an `awaitingCacheSync` flag until the cache reflects the write.

title: Destructive key cleanup ran on cancelled/transient auth, not just true loss
learning: `cleanupSinglePrivateKey` treated any falsy `getPrivateKey()` as a successful cleanup and force-removed the signer key without auth, but `getPrivateKey` returns `undefined` for cancelled biometric prompts and transient lockouts too — so a user who cancelled silently lost their signer. A separate path pre-deleted the stored key before re-import, risking data loss if the store then failed. Gate irreversible/destructive operations on the specific confirmed cause (e.g. a caught `BiometryInvalidationError`), preserve the prior error path for ambiguous/transient failures, and never delete existing data before its replacement is durably stored.

title: Bare RTK Query tag invalidated every safe/space on any mutation
learning: `providesTags: ['SafeOverviews']` / a global `invalidateTags(['SafeOverviews'])` on every `TxEvent.SUCCESS` invalidated every overview query across all chains and safes, causing refetch storms. Scope cache tags per entity id (`[{ type: 'SafeOverviews', id: makeSafeOverviewTag(chainId, address) }]`) and invalidate only the affected id, so a mutation on one safe/space doesn't refetch all of them. The same applies to coarse `providesTags/invalidatesTags: ['surveys']`-style tags.

title: Analytics events hardcoded/reused a label across multiple entry points
learning: A shared `AddAccountsChooser` hardcoded `entry_point: 'dashboard'` while also being rendered from the Safe Accounts page, and a Security-tab CTA reused `SPACE_LABELS.space_dashboard_card` — both collapsed distinct funnels into one bucket and mis-attributed analytics. When a shared component or CTA fires an analytics event from more than one surface, pass the entry-point/label in as a prop and add a dedicated label per surface; never hardcode one surface's value or reuse another page's label.
